dc-record.title: Map
dc-record.description:
This map was part of a six-fold brochure produced by North Shore Ralroad to promote the sale of lots in Monte Rio, Mesa Grande (now Villa Grande), Monte Rio Park (Starret Hill), and Monte Rio Addition (the Terraces).
The verso of the map contains six panels: Monte Rio Park "Country Home Colony on the Russian River" A Choice of Lots Ranging from $50 to $100 each. Photo of four cottages in the Redwoods. Monte Rio Additon "Placed ont eh Market April 8th, 1905" 50 Lots Sold on Opening Day at $50,00 to $150.00 Two photos of Russian River, upper showing large beach in Mesa Grande (now Patterson Point Preserve; lower showing view of river and deforested hills. Sunny Summer Homes "Excursion Rates: and Home Sites for only $50 each Two photos: one of Monte Rio (train) Station and one of Monte Rio Hotel Homes at Monte Rio: Photo of cottages on hill Mesa Grande Park: Aerial photo of Mesa Grande from Moscow Road to River, view of river in distance Homes at Sheridan: Photo of homes west of Monte Rio Park on South side of river (Sheridan not shown on map)
dc-record.subject: Map

dc-record.title: Tape, Magnetic
dc-record.description:
A digital TIF file created by Michael Cochran on Sept. 5, 2012.
The map was part of a six-fold brochure produced by North Shore Ralroad to promote the sale of lots in Monte Rio, Mesa Grande (now Villa Grande), Monte Rio Park (Starret Hill), and Monte Rio Addition (the Terraces).
Note: John Schubert found this map of Starrett Hill & Mesa Grande a whioe back and made a scan for me. I have since cleaned it up and inserted a parchment backgrouind. It's been reshot professionally as a tip file. I have been meaning to send this you you for a while, but keep forgetting to do so. I was going to email the image, but it was too large. Its a great map and I wanted other people to be able to have (it) too. Regards, Michael Cochran
dc-record.subject: Tape, Magnetic

dc-record.title: Monte Rio Railroad Station, April 16, 1909
postcard
dc-record.description:
Postcard Russian River Station at Monte Rio. April 16, 1909. Verso: Dear Bess_ Arrived safely. It was fine yesterday but is rather cold and windy today. It's town is nearly deserted. Yours, May Miss Bess Marshall 541 Liberty St. San Francisco
Laminated copy in oversized bin

postcard

dc-record.title: Monte Rio Vacation Wonderland
Map
dc-record.description:
"An all-in-one FIELD GUIDE to a weekend in + around Monte Rio Vacation Wonderland and teh RIO Theater & Cafe.
Created in January, 2015, this fold-out map provided three panels of Weekend #1, #2, & #3 options. Other panels include "Places to Stay", "Where to East & Drink" and "What to Do". A hand-drawn map shows the roads and names of towns from Jenner East to to Healdsburg, and from Cazadero South to Point Reyes & Petaluma/

dc-record.title: Official Map: Sonoma County 1983
Map
dc-record.description:
Folded map of Sonoma County with Points of Interest copyright 1970 by Compass Maps, Inc, Modesto, Ca. 1983 edition
Verso has multiple panels of information: Welcome to Sonoma County: Industry Maps of "Sonoma County industrial Areas" Map Title Page Sonoma County Location Sonoma County History Chambers of Commerce The Communities (date of establishment and populations of Santa Rosa, Rohnert Park, Petaluma, Cotati, Sebastopol, Healdsburg, Clorerdale, Sonoma) Culture and Recreation Economic Development Board
dc-record.subject: Map

dc-record.title: The Russian River and the Sonoma Coast 1982
Map
dc-record.description: A map provided by the Russian River Chamber of Commerce showing towns and roads from Forestville/Mirabel Park to Villa Grande. Also Coastal roads from Sea Ranch to Gualala. Includes Membership Directory, and many business card/advertisements: Balalaika Cottages, Burl Shop, Camelot Resort, River Village, Arcado's market, Fif'e's Resort/Bar/Restaurant, the Willows, Bank of America, Alan Claman, Attorney, Bridgehaven, Russian River Realty, Hernandez Realty, River end Campground, Murphy's Jenner by the Sea, Triple R Bar & Resort, Leonard Matloviche's Stumptown Annie's, Abby's Playland Park, Casa de joanna, Sleepy Hollow Resort, Perry's Delli, Southside Resourt, & Donovan's Resort, . Verso of map has many local business card/advertisements: Cazanoma Lodge, WC Trowbridge Canoe Trips, Midway Deli, Raford House, Embers, Blue Heron Inn, Jack Wright Realty, Salt Point Lodge, Ring Canyon Camp Ground, Schoolhouse Lodge, Angelo's Resort/Restaurant, Burke's, Buck's, Rainbow Cattle Co., Highlands Resort, Skippy's Hacienda Inn, North Wood Properties, Mama Mia Pizza Cafe, Burdon's Restaurant, Village Inn, Bodega Bay Lodge, Woods, Torr Realty, Casini Ranch, Timber Cove Inn/Restaurant/Gallery. List community events, Camping & Recreation Areas and things to do by season.
dc-record.subject: Map

dc-record.title: Pulvers Auto Road Map: San Francisco to Crescent City via Coast Route
Map
dc-record.description:
Pre Golden Gate Bridge and route 101, this map shows roads from San Franciso north to Ukiak, from Fairfield and Clear Lake west to the Ocean. A second map show coastal routes from Point Area and Lake Port to Crescent City.
Verso shows ads for Guerneville Hotel, Harry Harris Groceries, Albert's Grove, Starrett market, Ferngrove, Robertson Bros. Garage, New Monte Rio Hotel, Monte Rio Garage, Lark & Warne Drugs, Russian Rever Camp Grounds,
Photos show "New Monte Rio-Guerneville Highway (no cars) and canoers at Guernewood Park ("Pronounced Gurnwood").
dc-record.subject: Map

Guernewood Park, CA
Large two story hotel and outbuildings (possibly rental cabins) on beach with large porch overlooking river. Eight umbrellas with patio tables. Stairs leading down to beach with concession stand and changing rooms with wooden walkway. Sunbathers and swimmers along beach. Kiddie pool roped off. Canoes on beach.

dc-record.title: Timecard
dc-record.description:
Time book showing record of time and wages of various workers with occupations listed as filer, choppper, teamster, barkler, 1st chain, 2nd chairn, sawyer, log cars, swamper. Many Italian names on some months. Other months show Chinese names without occupation titles.
Rest of time book was used for recipes including many desserts including Pound Cake, Potato Cake, Nut Cake, Lady Baltimore Cake, Spiced Loaf Cake, Chocolate Cake and Filling, Apple Fruit Cake, Dried Apple Cake and World's Fair Cake (1893 Chicago World's Fair introduced this first version of the brownie). Also recipes for pickles, tomato catsum and main dishes.
dc-record.subject: Timecard

dc-record.title: The Last Train from the Russian River
Clipping, Newspaper
dc-record.description:
"Russian River News" November 14, 1985 Article on 50th Anniverary of last train from the Russian River "At exactly 1:40 p.m. on Thursday, November 14, 1935 Conductor H.H. Johnson called 'All Aboard' and Engine 108 began to pull "The Last Train from the Russian River" Special center fold article shows Engine 108, the train schedule for the Guerneville Branch of the Northwestern Pacific Railroad, Korbel Train Station, the Duncan Mills train stations and a Map of the Guerneville Branch. The article recounts the history of extending the railroad to Guerneville and the demise of the narrow and broad gauge branches.
Original also in River News file.
dc-record.subject: Clipping, Newspaper
Railroads
Trains

dc-record.title: Monte Rio
Postcard, picture
dc-record.description: Sepia postcard with 9 people on Monte Rio beach. 1925 or 1926 Water Carnival. Queen's Court All in Italian Renaissance-theme costumes, men in breeches, capes, and feathered hats, women in ruffled layers, one man kneeling wearing court jester costume. In background is float with chair, and to left is man sitting in row boat with Chinese lantern. 5 postcards of various clarity. 1 print with names written on back. Rhea Foto. Leland Aher standing in back. Ferne Guidotti, Queen. Mrs. June La Franchi on left. Lorabelle Kuig on right. Leland Geisenhofer, Jester. Georgie Guidotti on left.
dc-record.subject: Postcard, picture
Water Carnival

dc-record.title: Views of Monte Rio
Print, Photographic
dc-record.description:
A postcard series "Compliments of Chas F. Carr, Montrio, Cal. containing eighteen small photos of Monte Rio from early 1900.
Board Walk and Depot, Monte Rio Boating on the Russian River (Launch Uno filled with people)\ Interior of Dancing Pavilion Looking Up the Railroad Track Annex rto the Montrio Hotel Rustic Bridge at Monte Rio Montrio Hotel, Chas. F. Carr, Prop Landing at Monte Rio North Shore Depot, Monte Rio General View of Monte Rio (with river steamer "Monte Rio" in background The arrival of the Train Arrival of the Daily Papers (by boat "Anona") View Looking down the River River Steamer "Monte Rio" River Scene at Monte Rio the "Bungalow" at Monte Rio Launch Party Starting Out (river launch "Uno" Looking down (railroad tracks) Russian River
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

dc-record.title: Postcard, picture
dc-record.description:
Sepia Postcard with 15 people on Monte Rio beach, 4 women, 1 man and 9 children of various ages. Other people in background. Back Row:Susie Hayes, Molly Breen, Mrs. McGrath, Mr.McGrath (Molly's brother),
Middle Row: McGrath boy, Mrs. Higgins

Ridley, Alice Ridley, Rea Ridley 1947: NA Ridley, Rea Ridley, John Ridely, Alice Ridley 1950: Rea Ridley, Josephine Girolo-Ridley, NA Ridley, Sharon & John Ridley 1967: Rea Ridley 2011: Heather L. dc-record.title: 1907 Ridley-Lawson Family Trip
Scrapbook
dc-record.description: Compiled by Sharon Ridley-Smith, this is a record of the Ridley and Lawson family vacation from Santa Rosa to Skaggs Spring Resort to Gualala to Sonoma Coast at Jenner to Austin Creek. The trip too more than a week. Since there were nine people, two wagons and four horses were used. Includes biographical information about Jesse Printice and Janus Fletcher. The record is illustrated with various family photographs. The original photographs are individually listed in the RRHS Photo Catalog.
dc-record.subject: Scrapbook

dc-record.format: Buff card stock 14" x 10 1/2" in brown cardboard scrapbook.
dc-record.title: Chamber of Commerce Auxiliary History Book
Scrapbook
dc-record.description:
1942- 1959 Scrapbook, Women's Auxiliary to the Monte Rio Chamber of Commerce.
Thirty-two pages, buff cardstock 14" x 10 1/2." Each page includes seven to ten newspaper articles from various newspapers along the Russian River. The articles chronicle the activities Women's Auxiliary as well as events in the area. Some articles merely discuss meetings including members in attendance or election of officers. Others articles relate the celebrations, luncheons, dinners, and parties hosted by the auxiliary. Some pictures from news articles or actual photos included. Also, included are some mementos.
dc-record.subject: Scrapbook
Women's Auxiliary, Chamber of Commerce Monte Rio
Monte Rio Fire Station

dc-record.title: Drawing
dc-record.description: A copy of a 1948 cartoon drawing done by Robert Samish and Earl Crawford. The original hangs in the Sportsman's Club in Duncans Mills. Focus is political sarcasm regarding the fate of the steelhead trout with drawing of sea lions, steelhead trout and many quotations, and a large tree of "Public Apathy" under which are tied politics, antique laws, warden, red tape, anti-conservation, and district judge. Located in Map Case.

Sportman's Club
Fishing
Duncans Mills

dc-record.description: Printed summary of items in 2202.35.003.An assortment of evryday items used by some of the earliest inhabitants of Guerneville. dc-record.title: Archive
dc-record.description: 001 BOX 1 File Folder 1 - Programs (single copy of programs, undated) File Folder 2 - Newsletters (1982-84) File Folder 3 - Reviews (includes news clippings about various shows) File Folder 4 - Flyers and Promos (a variety of materials advertising shows) File Folder 6 - Business Letters (Articles of Incorporation; lease agreements; resignation of General Director; fund raising correspondence from 1982-88) File Folder 7 - Grant/Cowell (documents pertaining to grant applications from 1984-87, including history of the theatre) File Folder 8 - Notes/Arts Council Grant (documents pertaining to preparation of grant applications from 1984, including interviews/notes from Regan Durrant and others) File Folder 9 - Posters for various shows (undated) File Folder 10 - Photo Montage from Three Penny Opera (undated) File Folder 11 - Loose clippings, designs and photographs from "The Wizard of Oz" (1996) Box of Slides - "Under Milk Wood" Binder 1 - Documents related to grant applications, leases, insurance and budgets (1982-87) Binder 2 - Scrapbook relating to "Twelfth Night" (1981) Binder 3 - Miscellaneous Photographs from "Bus Stop", "Early Hot Curtain", "Twelfth Night" Binder 4 - Miscellaneous Photographs from "Clue", "Dark of the Moon", "Under Milk Wood", One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest", "The Balcony", "Lysistrata", "A Midsummer Night's Dream" (1981-1982)
dc-record.subject: Archive
Plays
Theaters
Musicals

dc-record.title: Archive
dc-record.description: Archive Collection of Russian River and River Rep Theater BOX 2 File Folder 1 - Loose copies of photos of Regan Durrant (1981/82) File Folder 2- Envelope containing scripts of various skits (undated) File Folder 3 - "An Ode to Reiver Rep or Gone with the Wind in the Willows" (Terry's poem regarding the disbanding of repertoire) File Folder 4 - Box of Photo proofs of various shows (undated) Binder 1 - Press releases, Reviews, Posters and Programs (Dec. 1978-Aug. 1982) Binder 2 - Press releases, Reviews, Posters and Programs (Oct. 1982-1996), plus Regan's Obituary Binder 3 - Bylaws of organization; responsibilities of Producer, Sound Designer, Stage Manager
dc-record.subject: Archive

dc-record.title: Archive
dc-record.description:
Finding Aid: Guerneville Community Church Collection (2022.30) Donated by Megan Hope .001 Altar Cross: Location: Object case
BOX 1 Location: .002 Seal/stamp: .003 Original wooden collection bowls (2) .004 Colorful map of Lower Russian River (1948) .005 New New Testament Book, signed by Henry Hayden in 1944 to Verna Camper (Thomas Nelson & Sons copyright 1900) .006 Holy Bible, donated by anonymous person 1/12/1964 (Harper & Brothers copyright 1952) .007 Holy Bible extract, with inscription that the bible was destroyed by the 1964 flood .008 Poster, "Covenant of Call," signed by Elizabeth Hearn May 6, 2007 .009 Poster of Reverends serving the church (1895-2018) .010 Poster of plaques "In Memoriam"
BOX 2 .011 Typed church histories 1894-2022 and 1894-1960 .012 Awards: a. Floodfight '86 Certificate of Merit (recognizing flood recovery operations of February 1986, from the State of California Office of Emergency Services) b. Certificate of Appreciation, dated May 1, 1985, from Sonoma County Social Service Department .013 The Church Register (bound volume identifying Pastors 1895-2014, Active members, Baptisms and Deaths) .014 Treasurer's Account Book (bound volume, 1968-1974) .015 Four check records books (1956-57, 1958-59, 1971-72, 1973-76) .016 Minute Books, Board of Trustees (1945-1948 and 1948-1974, including By-laws) .017 Deacon and Deaconesses records (bound volume, 1948-1964, including amended By-laws) .018 Women's Fellowship - Minutes of Monthly Meetings (1957-1964) .019 Church records: Baptisms, affirmations, deceased from A-Z (bound volume) .020 Community Church Directories (softcover booklets, 1988, 1991, 1993, 1999) .021 File folder: news clippings a. Sonoma West Times & News Article regarding "church celebrates 50 years" (4/24/14) .022 File folder: Church flyers .023 File folder: Correspondence (general) a. Letter, dated May 23, 2022, donating historical items and records to RRHS, signed by Edy Sallee .024 File folder: Legal/financial documents .025 File folder: Photos (undated) .026 File folder: Building Program and related architectural drawings .027 File folder: Minutes of quarterly and annual church meetings (1954-1955, 1962-1980) .028 Promotional item--Guerneville Redwood Chapel, funeral director .029 Magnetic tape (2/19/67) re Dedication of New Church .030 Record of Member Certificates. .031 Church Guest Books (bound volumes, 1944-47, 1947-55, 1960-66, 1964-85, 1986-94)
------------------------------------------------------------
.032. Unboxed: Baptismal Font - eight sided, symbolizing new life. Dedicated to Judith Dougherty 1945-1948: Location: Store Room

dc-record.title: Sonoma County Sheriff's Office Special Bulletin
Bulletin
dc-record.description:
Sonoma County Sheriff's Office Special Bulletin Reward of $4,000 for information leading to the arrest and conviction of the person or persons responsible for the death of George Markarian.
On the evening of December 26, 1977, George Markarian was attacked in front of his Guerneville home. The attack hospitalized Mr. Markarian and eventually led to his death.

dc-record.title: Postcard, Picture
dc-record.description: Picture postcard of Monte Rio flood December 11-12, 1937 Three men on boat in the river verso-Highland Dell Hotel across river. dc-record.title: Patterson Point Conservation Easement Baseline Documentation
Report, Technical
dc-record.description: Report documenting physical features, land use, easements, and biological and hdrologic features on the Patterson Point Property relative to a Deed and Agreement conveying a conservation easement to the Sonoma County Agricultural Preservation and Open Space District. The easement is to preserve the important biotic and scenic values of the property. The documentation was prepared for the Sonoma County Agricultural Preservation & Open Space District by Roger Sternberg Forestry and Land Conservation Consulting Services. Includes aerial photos and maps.
dc-record.subject: Report, Technical
Sonoma County Agricultural Preservation & Open Space District
Patterson point Preserve

dc-record.title: Dam in Russian River, Guerneville, Cal.
Postcard
dc-record.description: View of river with dam in foreground. Written on front: "We are camping near this flag in the upper right hand corner of the postal." Written on back: "Dear friend, We are having a grand time up here, boating, eating swimming & walking. Gathered hazelnuts & berries this afternoon. Wish I could stay two weeks. Jennie." Addressed to: Miss Amelia Metyner, Randolph St, Napa, Cal.
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: H.C. Burke's Boat and Bath House Guerneville, Cal.
Postcard
dc-record.description: Four canoes with boaters in foreground, row of stalls along boardwalk, bridge in background.
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: Bath House - Guerneville, Cal. Belben & Upp, Photo.
Postcard
dc-record.description: View of boardwalk and row of stalls in foreground, bridge in background. Addressed to: Mrs J. Buss, 127 Maine St Vallejo Calif. Written on back: "Dear folks- Just a time to tell you that we are all O.K and enjoying it very much. Will write a letter later on. Yesterday we walked up to the lots that the folks own and they are all right. We go to bed at 9 o'clock each night and get up at 6 o'clock for the mornings are beautiful. With love Marie."
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: Johnson's Beach Guerneville, Calif.
Postcard
dc-record.description: View of bathers on Johnson's beach with canoes and boardwalk in foreground, slide in background. Sign in foreground says "Deep Water just off shore and around tower. Beginners keep inside roped area."
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: Johnson's Beach From Bon Fire Circle
Postcard
dc-record.description: View of Johnson's beach and people on benches with flag pole in background. Addressed to: "Mrs. Frank Parkhurst- 6432- Pine Haven Rd. Oakland, Cal." Written on back: "My dear: Just have a [unknown] until 20th. All I could get till then, so will look for you to come see us after then. Had a grand time in your lovely home and we won't forget it [unknown] you. We liked your husband [unknown]. Pat talks about him constantly. Said he feels like calling you Aunt Marie and uncle Frank somehow. Write me here. Also send Grace [unknown] address please. (Mrs. Chas. McCrary). Love, [unknown]"
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: 43. Russian River, Guerneville, California

dc-record.description: View of Johnson's beach and bathers with bridge in background.
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: Bathing Beach, Guerneville, Cal.
Postcard
dc-record.description: View of Johnson's beach with stalls, canoes and footbridge in foreground, bridge in background. Addressed to: "Mrs. Edith J. Smith #2945 Brookdale Ave. Oakland Calif." Written on back: "Well how is everybody? You will owe me two cards when you receive this! My mother & I had a pretty good time toether. I guess she has told you all about it by this time- we have been across this bridge that is on the card. My hubbie is very sick now, he has to stay in bed. I hope you will answer soon. Well I am going to bed now. Love to all I hope you read this. Mrs. L. W. [unknown]"
dc-record.subject: Postcard

dc-record.title: California Two - Footer The Western Railroader For the Western Railfan
Booklet
dc-record.description: Sixteen page booklet, Volume 32 No 11, Issue No 357 by Theodore G Wurm, with detailed history,description and photos of the "California two-footer" , the narrow-gauge railway system built for the Sonoma Magnesite Company in Sonoma County, California, which operated during the 1910s and early 1920s. This mining operation, often associated with the "Red Slide" deposits near Guerneville and Cazadero, was once the largest producer of magnesite in the United States.
dc-record.subject: Booklet
